title: Denver · The Recent Turn
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2020 + DPD incidents 2021–2026 · 78 neighborhoods · real locations

hook.line: Denver crime, down since the 2022 peak
hook.sub: DPD incidents · a real, recent decline — up close

punchline.sub: −20% in three years · reported criminal offenses (DPD), the same measure

copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2020 the gray marks are the FBI's classic 'index' crimes. From 2021 the figures are DPD's own incident data, which counts a much broader set of offenses, so the level steps up. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · DPD OPEN DATA
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location, block-level, sampled.
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 78 neighborhoods — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level). 99.9% map to one of 78 neighborhoods — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2020) and DPD incident data (2021+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Denver neighborhood reports the fewest Group A crimes?
copy.sourceLine: Data: Denver Police Department via Denver Open Data Catalog · real block-level locations
copy.coverageText: 99.9% of records mapped to a neighborhood
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Denver Police Department incident data (rolling 5 years + YTD) via the Denver Open Data Catalog & FBI UCR (1985–2020) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer (ORI CO0160000). Neighborhoods: City and County of Denver official statistical neighborhoods. DPD's source omits sex offenses; FBI UCR index crimes and DPD incidents are different measures, shown as two chapters and never equated. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1986: the FBI-era peak — 56,326 index crimes reported.
2008: the low point — 22,856, less than half the 1980s peak.
2020: back up to 40,949 — the last year of the FBI measure.
2022: 70,227 — the recent high under DPD's broader incident count.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — anonymized to the block by DPD open data.
May 2022 — the busiest Group A month of the last five years: 5,789.
Five Points — Denver's busiest neighborhood for reported Group A crime.
University Park: down 53% since 2022 — the city's biggest neighborhood drop.
Reported Group A is down citywide: 64,868 (2022) → 49,428 (2025), −24%.
